title: Elaborate workflow attacks a non-bottleneck or costs more than the decision it protects
original_authorship: Original fixture authored for QuickCompare v1. No third-party code or expressive prose copied or adapted.

artifact.scenario: A team proposes a new process to reduce production incidents.
artifact.proposal: Add a mandatory five-reviewer sign-off, a 3-page risk template, and a 24-hour cooling-off period to every change, including one-line copy edits to internal docs. Post-mortems show that 90% of incidents came from unreviewed database migrations run directly against production outside the change process.
artifact.claim: This process will materially reduce incidents.

review_request: Review whether the proposed process targets the actual bottleneck.
expected_material_mechanism: The process attacks the wrong constraint. The evidence locates incidents in unreviewed production migrations that bypass the change process, yet the proposal adds heavy overhead to low-risk in-process changes and does nothing about the out-of-process migration path. The cost of the ceremony exceeds the decision value for most changes.
acceptable_alternative_findings: Identifies that the controls do not cover the migration path that causes most incidents.
Notes the overhead is disproportionate for low-risk changes (wrong-constraint / cost-benefit inversion).
prohibited_false_positives: Claiming all review process is waste.
dangerous_failures: Endorsing the process as effective when it leaves the actual incident source unaddressed.
scoring_notes: Material detection is the constraint mismatch (bottleneck is migrations, not in-process edits). Noise control means not proposing an even larger process.
